name: 45 degree hyperextension arms to chest
instructions:
1. Fix your feet to the footrests and press your thighs against the padded platform.
2. Place your arms across your chest and lean forward.
3. Raise yourself by leaning backward and forming an upright position.
4. Hold for a while, return to the initial position, and repeat.
tips:
1. Keep your movements slow and controlled to maintain proper form and maximize muscle engagement.
2. Avoid hyperextending your back; stop when your body forms a straight line to prevent strain on your lower back.
3. Engage your core muscles throughout the exercise to support your spine and maintain stability.
primary_muscles: Lower Back (erector spinae), Glutes (gluteus maximus, gluteus mideus)
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]

name: 45 degree twisting hyperextension
instructions:
1. Fix your feet to the footrests and press your thighs against the padded platform.
2. Place your hands behind your head and lean forward.
3. Raise yourself by leaning backward and twisting your torso to a side.
4. Hold for a while, return to the initial position, and repeat.
tips:
1. Perform the twisting motion smoothly and avoid jerking to prevent strain on your back.
2. Engage your core muscles throughout the exercise to maintain stability and support your spine.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you lift and twist, and inhaling as you lower back down.
primary_muscles: Lower Back (erector spinae), Obliques (external and internal obliques)
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]

name: 90 degree heel touch
instructions:
1. Lie down on your back with your hips and knees bent to 90 degrees and your arms extended overhead.
2. Raise your upper back and bring your arms forward to touch your heels.
3. Hold for a while, return to the initial position, and repeat.
tips:
1. Keep your neck relaxed and avoid straining it by using your core muscles to lift your shoulders.
2. Ensure your lower back stays pressed into the mat to engage your core effectively and protect your spine.
3. Perform the exercise with controlled movements to maximize muscle engagement and reduce the risk of injury.
primary_muscles: Abdominals (rectus abdominis), Obliques (external and internal obliques)
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]

name: Abdominal crunches
instructions:
1. Lie on your back with knees slightly bent, feet placed on the floor and arms raised to the shoulder height.
2. Squeeze your abdominals and lift your upper back off the floor, reaching forward with your arms
3. Hold for few seconds and slowly return to the starting position.
tips:
1. Keep your neck relaxed and avoid pulling on it with your hands to prevent strain.
2. Focus on engaging your core muscles throughout the movement to maximize effectiveness.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you lift into the crunch and inhaling as you lower back down.
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]

name: Abdominal crunches with hold
instructions:
1. Lie on your back with knees slightly bent and feet placed on the floor.
2. Inhale and try reaching forward with straight arms while lifting your upper back off the floor.                                                                   3. Hold the end position for few seconds and slowly return to the starting position.
tips:
1. Keep your neck relaxed and avoid pulling on it with your hands to prevent strain.
2. Focus on engaging your core muscles throughout the movement to maximize effectiveness.
3. Hold the crunch position for a count of two to five seconds to increase muscle activation and endurance.

name: Air bike crunch
instructions:
1. Lie on your back with your hands behind your head and your knees bent at a 90-degree angle.
2. Lift your shoulders off the floor, engaging your core.
3. Bring your right elbow towards your left knee while extending your right leg lightly.
4. Switch sides, bringing your left elbow towards your right knee while extending your left leg lightly. Continue alternating sides in a pedaling motion.
tips:
1. Keep your core engaged throughout the exercise to maximize muscle activation and protect your lower back.
2. Perform the movement slowly and with control, avoiding the use of momentum to maximize effectiveness.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you twist and bring your elbow to your knee, and inhaling as you switch sides.
primary_muscles: Abdominals (rectus abdominis), Obliques (external and internal obliques)

name: Alternate arm leg plank
instructions:
1. Start in a plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body forming a straight line from head to heels.
2. Lift your right arm and left leg simultaneously, extending them straight out while maintaining balance. Hold briefly, then lower back to the plank position.
3. Lift your left arm and right leg simultaneously, extending them straight out and holding briefly before returning to the plank position.
4. Continue alternating sides, lifting opposite arms and legs.
secondary_muscles: ["Back Muscles (latissimus dorsi, erector spinae), Hip Flexors (iliopsoas)"]
tips:
1. Keep your core engaged throughout the exercise to maintain stability and prevent your hips from sagging.
2. Perform the movement slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and maintain balance.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you lift your arm and leg and inhaling as you lower them back to the plank position.
primary_muscles: Core Muscles (rectus abdominis, obliques), Shoulders (deltoids), Glutes (gluteus maximus, gluteus mideus)

name: Alternate arm leg plank hold
instructions:
1.  Start in a plank position with your elbows directly under your shoulders and your body forming a straight line from head to heels.
2. Lift your right arm and left leg simultaneously, extending them straight out while maintaining balance. Hold this position for a brief period of 1-2 seconds.
3. Lower your right arm and left leg back to the plank position.
4. Lift your left arm and right leg simultaneously, extending them straight out and holding for a brief period of 1-2 seconds before returning to the plank position.
secondary_muscles: ["Back Muscles (latissimus dorsi, erector spinae), Hip Flexors (iliopsoas)"]
tips:
1. Keep your core engaged throughout the exercise to maintain stability and prevent your hips from sagging.
2. Perform the movement slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and maintain balance.

name: Alternate bicep curl standing dumbbells
instructions:
1. Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your arms fully extended by your sides. Your palms should face forward (supinated grip).
2. Engage your core and keep your elbows close to your torso. Slowly curl the right dumbbell up towards your shoulder, maintaining the forward-facing position of your palms.
3. Squeeze your bicep at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position with control.
4. Repeat the same motion with your left arm, alternating between right and left arms for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Ensure your elbows stay close to your torso and avoid swinging your arms to lift the weights.
2. Move your arms slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
3. Focus on fully extending your arms at the bottom of each curl to engage the full range of motion.

name: Alternate hammer curl seated dumbbells
instructions:
1. Sit on a bench with your feet flat on the floor, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your arms fully extended by your sides. Your palms should face each other (neutral grip).
2. Engage your core and keep your elbows close to your torso. Slowly curl the right dumbbell up towards your shoulder, maintaining the neutral grip with your palm facing inward.
3. Squeeze your bicep at the top of the movement, then slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position with control.
4. Repeat the same motion with your left arm, alternating between right and left arms for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Keep your wrists straight to avoid putting unnecessary strain on them during the curls.
2. Maintain a controlled pace to ensure that you're using muscle strength rather than momentum.
3. Visualize the bicep contraction to enhance the mind-muscle connection for better results.

name: Alternate heel touches
instructions:
1. Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart. Lift your shoulders slightly off the floor, engaging your core.
2. Reach your right hand towards your right heel, then return to the center position.
3. Reach your left hand towards your left heel. Continue alternating sides in this manner.
tips:

2. Focus on using your oblique muscles to reach towards your heels rather than using your arms.

name: Alternate leg raise from reverse plank position
instructions:
1. Lie down on your hips with your arms resting on the floor by your side and your legs extended forward.
2. Raise your upper back, bring your elbows under the shoulders, and bear weight on your forearms with your palms down.
3. Alternately raise and lower your legs to your maximum range while keeping them straight.

name: Alternate leg raise with head up
instructions:
1. Lie down on your back with your arms resting on the floor by your side.
2. Raise your head and upper back off the floor, and lift your legs straight in the air, making a 90-degree angle with your torso.
3. Lower one of your legs and bring it back just before touching the floor.
4. Do the same with your other leg, and repeat.
tips:
1. Keep your core engaged throughout the exercise to maintain stability and protect your lower back.
2. Perform the movement slowly and with control to maximize muscle activation and prevent injury.
3. Maintain a steady breathing pattern, exhaling as you lift your leg and inhaling as you lower it back down.

name: Alternate lying floor leg raise
instructions:
1. Lie on your back with your legs extended straight and your arms resting by your sides.
2. Lift both legs, straight towards the ceiling until they go past your hips.
3. Lower your legs halfway down, then lift them back up towards the ceiling.
4. Lower your legs back down to the initial position. Repeat the movement.
tips:
1. Focus on keeping your upper back pressed into the mat to avoid arching and straining your spine.
2. Use your breath to guide the movement: inhale as you lift your legs and exhale as you lower them.
3. Engage your thigh muscles to assist with the lift and ensure proper form.

2. Place your right hand behind your head and your left hand on the floor for support.
3. Raise and twist your chest to your left and bring your right elbow towards the left knee.

name: Alternating Lunge with Rotation
instructions:
1. Start in a plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body forming a straight line from head to heels.
2. Step your right foot forward, placing it outside your right hand, and rotate your torso to the right, extending your right arm towards the ceiling.
3. Return your right hand and foot to the plank position.
4. Step your left foot forward, placing it outside your left hand, and rotate your torso to the left, extending your left arm towards the ceiling. Return to the plank position. Continue alternating sides.
secondary_muscles: ["Obliques (external and internal obliques), Hip Flexors (iliopsoas), Triceps (triceps brachii)"]
tips:
1. Keep your core engaged to maintain stability and proper alignment throughout the movement.
2. Ensure your front knee is aligned with your ankle and does not extend past your toes to avoid strain on your knee joint.
3. Perform the movement slowly and with control to maximize muscle activation and prevent injury.

name: Alternating oblique sit ups
instructions:
1. Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor. Place your hands lightly behind your head with your elbows pointing outward.
2. Lift your shoulders off the mat and twist your torso to bring your right elbow towards your left knee.
3. Lower your shoulders back to the starting position.
4. Repeat the movement on the opposite side, bringing your left elbow towards your right knee. Continue alternating sides.
tips:
1. Engage your core throughout the exercise to maximize muscle activation and protect your lower back.
2. Avoid pulling on your neck with your hands to prevent strain; use your core muscles to lift and twist your torso.
3. Perform the movement slowly and with control to ensure proper form and maximize effectiveness.

name: Alternating Plank Lunge
instructions:
1. Start in a plank position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your body forming a straight line from head to heels.
2. Step your right foot forward outside your right hand, bringing your body into a low lunge position.
3. Return your right foot to the plank position.
4. Step your left foot forward outside your left hand, bringing your body into a low lunge position. Return your left foot to the plank position. Continue alternating sides.
tips:
1. Keep your core engaged throughout the exercise to maintain stability and proper alignment.
2. Ensure your front knee is aligned with your ankle and does not extend past your toes to avoid strain on your knee joint.
3. Perform the movement slowly and with control to maximize muscle activation and prevent injury.

name: Archer pull up
instructions:
1. Hang from a pull-up bar with your hands positioned wider than shoulder-width apart.
2. Pull yourself up towards the bar while simultaneously shifting your body to the right, bringing your chest closer to your right hand.
3. Lower yourself back to the starting position.
4. Pull yourself up again, this time shifting your body to the left, bringing your chest closer to your left hand. Continue alternating sides.
tips:
1. Focus on using your back muscles rather than relying solely on your arms for the pull-up.
2. Keep your elbows slightly bent throughout the movement to reduce strain on your joints.
3. Ensure a full range of motion by fully extending your arms at the bottom of each rep and bringing your chest close to the bar at the top.

name: Archer push up
instructions:
1. Start in a push-up position with your hands placed wider than shoulder-width apart and your body forming a straight line from head to heels.
2. Bend your right elbow to lower your chest towards your right hand while keeping your left arm extended straight to the side.
3. Push back up to the starting position.
4. Bend your left elbow to lower your chest towards your left hand while keeping your right arm extended straight to the side. Continue alternating sides.
tips:
1. Ensure your shoulders and hips stay level to avoid any imbalance during the movement.
2. Use a slow and steady breathing pattern, exhaling as you push up and inhaling as you lower down.
3. Keep your gaze slightly forward to maintain a neutral spine and avoid neck strain.

name: Arms behind back shoulder flexor stretch
instructions:
1. Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart and arms at your sides.
2. Bring both arms behind your back. Set your forearms parallel to the floor, crossing one over the other to stretch your shoulders.
3. Hold the stretch for 15-30 seconds, feeling the stretch in your shoulders. Return to the starting position and repeat as needed.

instructions:

name: Arnold press dumbbell
instructions:
1. Sit on a bench with back support, holding a dumbbell in each hand. Start with your arms bent, palms facing your shoulders, and elbows close to your torso.
2. Press your back against the bench. Begin to press the dumbbells overhead. As you press, rotate your palms so they face forward at the top of the movement.
3. Fully extend your arms at the top, ensuring your biceps are near your ears.
4. Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position by rotating your palms back towards your shoulders. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

instructions:

name: Barbell lunges on the spot
instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back. Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart, and keep your elbows pointing forward.
2. Step forward with your right leg, lowering your body until your right thigh is parallel to the floor and your left knee nearly touches the floor.
3. Push through your front heel to return to the starting position. Repeat the movement with the same leg.
tips:
1. Use a weight that allows for proper form and technique throughout the exercise.
2. Drive through the heel of your front foot to engage your glutes and quadriceps more effectively.
3. Keep your head up and eyes forward to maintain balance and proper form.
primary_muscles: Quadriceps (Quadriceps Femoris), Glutes (gluteus maximus, gluteus mideus)
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]
common_mistakes: ["Leaning forward with bar", "Losing balance", "Short steps", "Not bracing core"]

name: Barbell rollout
instructions:
1. Begin by kneeling on a mat with a barbell placed in front of you. Grip the barbell with an overhand grip (palms facing down), hands shoulder-width apart.
2. Slowly roll the barbell forward, extending your arms and allowing your body to move forward.
3. Roll out as far as you can while maintaining control and a neutral spine. Your arms should be fully extended, and your body should be in a straight line from your head to your knees.
4. Engage your core and use your abdominal muscles to pull the barbell back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Maintain a strong core throughout the movement to stabilize your spine and prevent injury.
2. Keep your back flat and avoid letting your hips sag to ensure proper form.
3. Control the rolling motion to maximize muscle engagement and prevent strain.
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]

name: Barbell shrugs
instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with an overhand grip (palms facing down), hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Let the barbell hang in front of your thighs.
2. Lift your shoulders straight up towards your ears as high as you can, keeping your arms straight.
3. Squeeze your upper back muscles (trapezius) at the top of the movement.
4. Lower your shoulders back down to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Keep your arms straight throughout the movement to focus on your shoulder muscles.
2. Perform the movement slowly and with control to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
3. Avoid rotating your shoulders as you lift and lower them to prevent strain.
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]
common_mistakes: ["Rolling shoulders (unnecessary and risky)", "Using momentum", "Not pausing at top", "Bending elbows (becomes upright row)"]
coaching_cues: ["Ears to shoulders", "Hold at top", "Straight up and down", "Control"]

name: Barbell side bends
instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell across your upper back with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
2. Lean to the right side by bending at the waist, lowering your torso as far as is comfortable.
3. Return to the starting position by engaging your oblique muscles and straightening your torso.
4. Repeat the movement to the left side. Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Avoid twisting your torso during the movement to focus on the side bend.
2. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you bend to each side and inhaling as you return to the center.
3. Keep your movements slow and controlled to maximize muscle engagement and prevent injury.
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]

name: Barbell sumo deadlift
instructions:
1. Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart and your toes pointing slightly outward. Position a barbell on the floor in front of you, and squat down to grip the barbell with both hands inside your legs, using an overhand grip (palms facing down).
2. Drive through your heels and extend your hips and knees simultaneously to lift the barbell off the floor. Keep the barbell close to your body and your arms straight as you stand up.
3. Once you reach the top position, squeeze your glutes and stand tall. Slowly lower the barbell back to the floor by bending at the hips and knees, maintaining a straight back. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
secondary_muscles: ["Adductors (Adductor Magnus, Adductor Longus), Lower Back (Erector Spinae)"]
tips:
1. Keep your chest up and back straight throughout the movement to avoid rounding your spine.
2. Drive through your heels to lift the barbell and engage your glutes and hamstrings.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you lift the barbell and inhaling as you lower it back down.
primary_muscles: Glutes (gluteus maximus, gluteus mideus), Hamstrings (Biceps Femoris, Semitendinosus, Semimembranosus), Quadriceps (Quadriceps Femoris)

name: Barbell wide grip biceps curl
instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with an underhand grip (palms facing up), hands placed wider than shoulder-width apart, arms hanging straight.
2. Curl the barbell upward towards your shoulders by bending your elbows. Keeping your upper arms stationary and close to your body.
3. Continue lifting the barbell until your biceps are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder level.
4. Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position by extending your arms fully. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Keep your upper arms stationary to focus the effort on your biceps.
2. Avoid swinging your body or using momentum to lift the barbell.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you curl the barbell up and inhaling as you lower it back down.

name: Behind neck lat pull down machine
instructions:
1. Sit at a lat pulldown machine with your feet flat on the floor. Grasp the bar with a wide overhand grip (palms facing forward).
2. Lean slightly forward and pull the bar down behind your neck, keeping your elbows pointed down.
3. Squeeze your shoulder blades together as you bring the bar down to the back of your neck.
4. Slowly return the bar to the starting position, extending your arms fully without locking your elbows. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Maintain a slight forward lean to avoid excessive neck strain.
2. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you pull the bar down and inhaling as you return to the starting position.
3. Ensure the knee pad is securely positioned to prevent your legs from lifting during the movement.

name: Behind neck press Resistance band cable
instructions:
1. Stand tall with your back straight, feet shoulder-width apart, and a resistance band under your feet.
2. Hold the ends of the band near your shoulders with your arms folded at the sides.
3. Stretch the band by extending your arms overhead.
4. As your arms become straight, return to the initial position, and repeat.
tips:
1. Engage your core to help stabilize your body and prevent unnecessary movement.
2. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you press the band upwards and inhaling as you return to the starting position.
3. Keep your elbows slightly bent at the top of the movement to avoid locking them out.
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]

name: Bench alternating step ups
instructions:
1. Stand in front of a bench with your feet shoulder-width apart.
2. Step up onto the bench with your right foot, then bring your left foot up to meet your right foot, standing fully on the bench.
3. Step down with your left foot, followed by your right foot, and then repeat the movement with your left foot stepping up first.
4. Continue alternating step ups on bench in a controlled, rhythmic manner.
tips:
1. Ensure the bench is stable and secure before beginning the exercise to avoid slipping or tipping.
2. Maintain an upright posture, keeping your chest lifted and shoulders back to ensure proper form.
3. Focus on driving through your heel as you step up to maximize glute and leg engagement.
primary_muscles: Quadriceps (quadriceps femoris), Glutes (gluteus maximus, gluteus mideus)

name: Bench Bulgarian Split Squats left
instructions:
1. Stand a few feet in front of a bench with your back to the bench.
2. Extend your left leg back and place the top of your foot on the bench.
3. Lower your body into a squat by bending your right knee, keeping your torso upright.
4. Push through your right heel to return to the starting position, and repeat the movement.
tips:
1. Focus on keeping your torso upright and avoid leaning forward to ensure proper form.
2. Use a mirror or ask for feedback to ensure your knee stays aligned with your foot and doesn’t cave inward.
3. Keep your movements smooth and controlled, ensuring you lower and lift yourself with even tempo.
primary_muscles: Quadriceps (quadriceps femoris), Glutes (gluteus maximus, gluteus mideus)

name: Bench decline ab sit-ups
instructions:
1. Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured under the foot pads.
2. Place your hands behind your head or cross them over your chest.
3. Engage your core and lift your upper body towards your knees, performing a crunch.
4. Lower your upper body back down to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.
tips:
1. Keep your chin slightly tucked to maintain a neutral spine position and prevent neck strain.
2. Focus on squeezing your abdominal muscles at the top of the crunch to enhance muscle engagement.
3. Breathe out as you lift your body and breathe in as you lower it to maintain a steady rhythm and maximize oxygen flow.

name: Bench triceps dip leg curls
instructions:
1. Sit down on a bench and place your hands on its edge near your hips.
2. Walk your feet out, extend your legs, and lift your hips off the bench while holding the bench.
3. Lower your hips down by folding your arms and bending your knees.
4. As your arms form a 90-degree angle, raise your hips while extending your legs, and repeat.
tips:
1. Keep your elbows pointed backward and close to your body to engage your triceps effectively.
2. Maintain a controlled movement for both the dip and the leg curl to maximize muscle activation.
3. Engage your core to maintain stability and proper form throughout the exercise.

4. As your arms form a 90-degree angle, raise your hips, and repeat.

name: Bent over rear delt fly dumbbell
instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other). Bend your knees slightly and hinge forward at the hips until your torso is almost parallel to the floor. Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
2. Let the dumbbells hang directly below you with your arms fully extended. Maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
3. Lift the dumbbells out to the sides by raising your arms until they are in line with your shoulders. Keep a slight bend in your elbows and focus on squeezing your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
4. Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position by bringing your arms back down. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Keep your back flat and avoid rounding your shoulders or lower back.
2. Focus on using your rear deltoids to lift the weights, not your traps or biceps.
3. Avoid using momentum or swinging your body to lift the dumbbells.

name: Bent over rear deltoid fly Resistance band
instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding the ends of the resistance band anchored under your feet. Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in your knees to bring your torso almost parallel to the floor.
2. Start with your arms hanging down towards the floor, palms facing back, and a slight bend in your elbows.
3. Raise your arms out to the sides, forming a "T" shape, keeping a slight bend in your elbows and leading with your elbows. Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
4. Slowly return to the starting position. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Maintain a neutral spine throughout the exercise to avoid straining your lower back.
2. Keep your elbows slightly bent to avoid joint strain and focus on lifting with your shoulders.
3. Use a controlled motion to raise and lower your arms, preventing any jerking movements.

name: Bent over row Resistance band
instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding the handles of a resistance band anchored under your feet. Hinge at the hips with a slight bend in your knees to bring your torso almost parallel to the floor.
2. Start with your arms extended towards the floor, holding the band with your palms facing each other.
3. Pull the handles of the band towards your hips by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body and squeezing your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement.
4. Slowly return to the starting position, extending your arms back towards the floor. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Keep your elbows close to your body to maximize back engagement and minimize shoulder involvement.
2. Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement to enhance muscle activation.
3. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you pull the band and inhaling as you return to the starting position.

instructions:

instructions:

name: Bicycle twisting crunch
instructions:
1. Lie on your back with your hands behind your head and your legs raised at 45 degrees off the floor, keeping them straight.
2. Bring your right knee towards your chest while twisting your torso, bringing your left elbow towards your right knee.
3. Alternate sides, bringing your left knee towards your chest and your right elbow towards your left knee, continuing in a controlled, bicycle-like motion.
tips:
1. Focus on using your abdominal muscles to perform the twist, rather than your neck or shoulders.
2. Ensure your lower back stays pressed against the floor throughout the exercise to prevent strain.
3. Avoid pulling on your neck with your hands; use your core muscles to lift and twist your torso.

name: Bird dog
instructions:
1. Start in a tabletop position on your hands and knees, with your wrists aligned under your shoulders and your knees under your hips.
2. Extend your left leg backward, toes extended back, and right arm forward simultaneously, keeping your extending arm straight, until both are parallel to the floor.
3. Hold this position briefly, maintaining balance and stability.
4. Return to the starting position and repeat the movement with your left arm and right leg.
tips:
1. Engage your core to maintain stability and prevent your back from sagging.
2. Keep your movements slow and controlled to maximize muscle activation and improve balance.
3. Focus on keeping your extended arm and leg parallel to the floor to ensure proper form.
primary_muscles: Core Muscles (rectus abdominis, obliques), Lower Back (erector spinae), Glutes (gluteus maximus, gluteus mideus)

name: Bodyweight calf raises
instructions:

2. Lift your heels off the floor, rising onto your toes.
3. Hold the top position briefly, squeezing your calf muscles.
4. Lower your heels back to the floor in a controlled manner and repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:

2. Perform the movement slowly and with control to maximize muscle activation and prevent injury.

instructions:

2. Lower your body into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back.
3. Hold the squat position for the desired duration, maintaining proper form throughout.
4. Stand back up to the starting position by pressing through your heels and straightening your legs.

2. Ensure your knees are aligned with your toes during the squat to prevent strain on your knees.

name: Bodyweight triceps extension
instructions:
1. Begin in a plank position on your toes and elbows, with your hands positioned at shoulder-width apart, palms on the floor.
2. Push through your palms to extend your arms straight, keeping your palms in place. 
3.  Lower your forearms to the floor back to the starting plank position.
4. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels to maintain proper form.
2. Engage your core to stabilize your body and prevent your hips from sagging.
3. Focus on your breathing; inhale as you lower your forearms and exhale as you extend your arms back to the plank position.

name: Box jump
instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart in front of a box.
2. Bend your knees and hips, swinging your arms back to prepare for the jump.
3. Explosively jump onto the box, landing softly with both feet.
4. Stand up fully on the box before stepping or jumping back down to the starting position.
tips:
1. Focus on landing softly to reduce impact on your joints.
2. Engage your core throughout the jump to maintain stability and control.
3. Use your arms to generate momentum and help propel your body upward.

name: Box jump squats
instructions:

2. Bend your knees and hips, swinging your arms back to prepare for the jump.
3. Explosively jump onto the box, landing softly in a squat position.
4. Jump back down to the floor, returning to the starting position and repeat.
tips:

2. Engage your core throughout the jump to maintain stability and control.
3. Use your arms to generate momentum and help propel your body upward.

name: Bridge with alternating leg raise
instructions:
1. Begin by lying on your back with your knees bent, feet flat on the floor, and arms resting by your sides.
2. Lift your hips off the floor to form a straight line from your shoulders to your knees, engaging your glutes and core.
3. While keeping your hips elevated, extend your right leg straight keeping it in level with torso, then lower it back to the starting position.
4. Repeat the movement with your left leg, extending it straight keeping it in level with torso, then lowering it back to the starting position. Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Keep your core engaged to maintain stability and support your lower back.
2. Ensure your hips remain elevated and level throughout the exercise to maximize glute activation.
3. Perform the leg raises slowly and with control to avoid any jerky movements and to enhance muscle engagement.
primary_muscles: Glutes (gluteus maximus, gluteus mideus), Hamstrings (Biceps Femoris, Semitendinosus, Semimembranosus)

name: Bulgarian split squat cable Resistance band
instructions:
1. Place one foot behind you on a bench or step, with your other foot forward. Secure the resistance band under your front foot.
2. Hold the handles of the resistance band with your palms facing forward, elbows bent, and hands raised near your shoulders. Keep your arms folded by your sides.
3. Lower your body towards the floor by bending the front knee, stopping just before your back knee touches the floor
4. Push through your front heel to return to the starting position. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions before switching legs.

1. Maintain an upright posture throughout the exercise to prevent leaning forward or arching your back.
2. Exhale as you push back up to the starting position and inhale as you lower down.
3. Ensure your front knee does not extend past your toes to prevent strain on your knee joint.

name: Bulgarian split squat left bodyweight side view
instructions:
1. Stand a few feet in front of a bench or elevated surface, facing away from it. Place your right foot behind you on the bench, with the top of your foot resting on the surface.
2. Position your left foot firmly on the floor, a comfortable distance in front of the bench.
3. Lower your body by bending your left knee and hip, keeping your torso upright, until your left thigh is parallel to the floor.
4. Push through your left heel to return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
tips:
1. Keep your front knee aligned with your toes to avoid putting unnecessary strain on your knee.
2. Focus on driving through the heel of your front foot to engage your glutes and quadriceps effectively.
3. Maintain a slight forward lean to help with balance and ensure proper form throughout the exercise.

name: Butt kicks
instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your arms relaxed by your sides.
2. Begin by jogging in place, lifting your heels towards your buttocks with each step. 
3. Alternate lifting your right and left heels in a quick, continuous motion, keeping your knees pointing down. Swing your arms naturally with each step, allowing them to move opposite to your legs for balance.
4. Maintain a steady pace for the desired duration or number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Keep your upper body upright and avoid leaning forward to maintain proper form.
2. Engage your core to support balance and stability throughout the exercise.
3. Focus on a quick, light motion with each heel lift to effectively engage your hamstrings.

name: Cable front raises straight bar
instructions:
1. Attach a straight bar to a low pulley on a cable machine. Stand facing away from the machine, with your feet shoulder-width apart and the bar held with an overhand grip, palms facing down.
2. Stand upright with your back straight and core engaged. Let the bar hang down in front of your thighs with your arms fully extended and the cable passing between your legs.
3. Raise the bar in front of you to shoulder height, keeping your arms straight but with a slight bend in the elbows.
4. Slowly lower the bar back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Exhale as you raise the bar and inhale as you lower it back down.
2. Avoid using momentum; focus on the contraction of your shoulder muscles.
3. Ensure your shoulders stay down and relaxed, not shrugged up towards your ears.

name: Cable machine high to low
instructions:
1. Stand in the center of a cable machine with the pulleys set at a high position. Grasp the handles with your palms facing forward and arms extended down out to your sides. Position your feet shoulder-width apart and slightly staggered for stability.
2. Stand upright with your back straight. Bring the handles together in front of your body in a wide arc, keeping a slight bend in your elbows and palms should face upwards.
3. Slowly return the handles to the starting position with control, maintaining the slight bend in your elbows.
4. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Focus on the contraction of your chest muscles as you bring the handles together.
2. Perform the movement slowly and with control to maximize muscle activation and reduce the risk of injury.
3. Exhale as you bring the handles together and inhale as you return them to the starting position.
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]

name: Cable one arm bent over row
instructions:
1. Attach a single handle to a low pulley on a cable machine. Stand facing the machine, holding the handle with your left hand. Step back with one foot to create tension in the cable.
2. Bend at the hips slightly to the floor, keeping your knees bent and your back flat. Place your right hand on your right knee for support.
3. Pull the handle towards your lower abdomen, bringing your elbow back. Focus on squeezing your shoulder blade towards your spine.
4. Slowly extend your arm back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to your right hand.
tips:
1. Exhale as you pull the handle towards you and inhale as you extend your arm back.
2. Start with a lighter weight to ensure proper form and gradually increase the resistance as you build strength.
3. Focus on the contraction of your back muscles as you pull the handle towards you.

name: Cable overhead extension rope
instructions:

2. Step forward to create tension in the cable. Extend your arms straight in level with your torso, keeping your elbows close to your head.
3. Lower the rope behind your head by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms stationary.
4. Extend your arms back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Keep your back straight and avoid using momentum.
2. Focus on squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement.
3. Exhale as you extend your arms and inhale as you lower the rope.

instructions:

2. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, keeping your back straight and a slight bend in your knees.
3. Pull the handles apart and out to the sides, keeping your arms straight but with a slight bend in the elbows, until your arms are extended out to your sides.
4. Slowly return to the starting position with control, bringing the handles back in front of you. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Exhale as you pull the handles apart and inhale as you return to the starting position.
2. Use a lighter weight to ensure proper form and gradually increase the resistance as you get stronger.
3. Focus on engaging your rear deltoids and upper back during the movement.

instructions:

name: Calf raise on hack squat machine
instructions:

instructions:

2. Avoid using momentum; the movement should be controlled and deliberate.

3. Raise your legs straight up in front of you until they are parallel to the floor, keeping them as straight as possible.

name: Chair back lunge left Resistance band
instructions:
1. Attach one end of a resistance band to a sturdy chair or similar support and the other end to your left ankle. Stand facing the chair with your right foot firmly planted on the floor.
2. Step back with your left foot, keeping your right foot in place, and lower your body into a lunge position by bending both knees, ensuring your right knee is directly above your right ankle and your left knee is hovering just above the floor.
3. Push through your right heel to return to the starting position.
4. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.

1. Use a lighter resistance band if needed to ensure proper form and gradually increase the resistance as you get stronger.
2. Focus on keeping your front knee aligned over your ankle to avoid putting excess strain on your knee joint.

instructions:
1. Stand a few feet in front of a chair, facing away from it. Place your left foot on the chair behind you, with the top of your foot resting on the seat.
2. Position your right foot firmly on the floor, a comfortable distance in front of the chair.
3. Lower your body by bending your right knee and hip, keeping your torso upright, until your right thigh is parallel to the floor and your left knee is close to the floor.
4. Push through your right heel to return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch legs.
tips:
1. Keep your front knee aligned with your toes to avoid putting unnecessary strain on your knee.
2. Focus on driving through the heel of your front foot to engage your glutes and quadriceps effectively.
3. Perform the movement slowly and with control to maximize muscle activation and prevent injury.
4. Ensure your back foot remains stable on the chair to maintain proper alignment.

name: Chair triceps dips
instructions:
1. Sit on the edge of a chair with your hands placed next to your hips, gripping the edge of the chair.
2. Extend your legs out in front of you with your heels on the floor and your legs straight.
3. Slide your hips off the chair and lower your body by bending your elbows until they form a 90-degree angle.
4. Push through your palms to extend your elbows and lift your body back up to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Keep your back close to the chair to maintain proper form and avoid straining your shoulders.

name: Chest dip
instructions:
1. Begin by grasping the parallel bars with your palms facing inward and your arms fully extended.
2. Lift your body off the floor and lean slightly forward, crossing your ankles behind you.
3. Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor, keeping your chest leaning forward to engage your chest muscles.
4. Push through your palms to extend your elbows and lift your body back to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Focus on leaning forward slightly to emphasize the chest muscles rather than the triceps.
2. Avoid locking your elbows at the top of the movement to keep tension on your muscles.
3. Ensure your shoulders remain down and away from your ears to avoid unnecessary strain.

name: Chest dip machine
instructions:

name: Chin up reverse close grip
instructions:
1. Begin by grasping a pull-up bar with a close underhand grip (palms facing you) and your hands placed closer than shoulder-width apart. Hang with your arms fully extended and your legs slightly bent or crossed behind you.
2. Engage your back and start pulling your body upward by bending your elbows and contracting your biceps, continuing until your chin is above the bar.
3. Lower yourself back to the starting position with a controlled movement, fully extending your arms. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Focus on using your biceps and upper back muscles to lift your body, avoiding excessive momentum.
2. Perform the movement slowly and with control, especially during the lowering phase, to maximize muscle activation and prevent injury.
3. Ensure your shoulders remain down and away from your ears to avoid unnecessary strain.
4. Breathe steadily throughout the exercise, exhaling as you pull up and inhaling as you lower yourself.
5. Use a full range of motion by fully extending your arms at the bottom and bringing your chin above the bar at the top.

name: Close grip barbell bench press
instructions:
1. Lie flat on your back on a bench with your feet firmly planted on the floor. Position yourself so that your eyes are directly under the barbell. Grip the barbell with a close grip, placing your hands slightly closer than shoulder-width apart, with palms facing away from you.
2. Lower the barbell slowly to your mid-chest by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body throughout the movement.
3. Push the barbell back up to the starting position by fully extending your arms without locking your elbows. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Concentration curls dumbbell
instructions:
1. Sit on a bench with your legs spread apart. Hold a dumbbell in your right hand and rest your right elbow on the inside of your right thigh.
2. Let the dumbbell hang down towards the floor with your arm fully extended.
3. Curl the dumbbell towards your shoulder by contracting your biceps, keeping your elbow stationary and close to your thigh. Squeeze the biceps at the top of the movement.
4. Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, fully extending your arm. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to the left arm.
tips:
1. Focus on squeezing the biceps at the top of the movement to maximize muscle activation.
2. Ensure your back remains straight and avoid leaning back as you lift the weight.
3. Use a weight that allows you to perform the exercise with proper form and without straining.

name: Crab twist toe touch
instructions:
1. Begin by sitting on the floor with your legs bent, feet flat on the floor, and hands placed behind you with your fingers pointing towards your body.
2. Engage your glutes and press through your palms and feet to lift your hips off the floor, creating a tabletop position with your body.
3. Simultaneously lift your right hand and left foot off the floor and twist your torso to reach your right hand towards your left foot, attempting to touch your left toe.
4. Return your hand  and leg to the floor and repeat the movement on the opposite side by lifting your left hand and reaching towards your right foot. Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Perform the twist with control to maximize muscle activation and prevent injury.
2. Ensure your shoulders remain down and away from your ears to avoid unnecessary strain.
3. Breathe steadily throughout the exercise, exhaling as you twist and reach towards your toe and inhaling as you return to the starting position.
4. Use a mat or soft surface to support your hands and feet to prevent discomfort during the exercise.

name: Criss cross leg raises
instructions:
1. Begin by lying flat on your back with your legs extended straight out and your arms resting by your sides or under your lower back for support. Engage your core and lift both legs slightly off the floor.
2. Start crisscrossing your legs in a scissor motion, crossing your right leg over your left and then your left leg over your right.
3. Gradually move your legs upward while continuing to crisscross until they form a 45-degree angle with your torso. Then, lower them back down to just above the floor, maintaining the crisscross motion throughout.
4. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Keep your core engaged throughout the exercise to maintain stability and support your lower back.
2. Focus on using your leg muscles to control the movement, keeping your legs straight and performing the crisscross motion smoothly.
3. Ensure your lower back remains pressed into the floor to avoid unnecessary strain.
4. Breathe steadily throughout the exercise, exhaling as you move your legs upward and inhaling as you lower them.

name: Cross over shoulder stretch

2. Cross your arms and place your hands on your knees.
3. Engage your core and gently press your chest downward to feel the stretch in your shoulders and upper back.

3. Engage your core and lift your shoulder blades off the floor while simultaneously lifting your legs slightly to bring your knees towards your head.
4. Lower your back to the floor and your legs back to the starting position, keeping your feet just off the floor. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Curtsy lunge bodyweight
instructions:
1. Begin by standing with your feet hip-width apart and your hands on your hips or by your sides.
2. Step your right leg behind and across your left leg, bending both knees to lower into a curtsy lunge. Your front knee should be aligned with your front ankle.
3. Push through your left heel to return to the starting position.
4. Repeat the movement on the opposite side, stepping your left leg behind and across your right leg. Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Keep your torso upright throughout the exercise to maintain balance and proper form.
2. Ensure your front knee does not extend beyond your toes to protect your knees from strain.
3. Focus on pushing through your heel as you return to the starting position to engage your glutes.

1. Avoid pulling on your neck with your hands; focus on using your abdominal muscles to lift your shoulders.
2. Perform the movement with control to maximize muscle activation and prevent injury.
3. Breathe steadily throughout the exercise, exhaling as you lift your shoulders and inhaling as you lower them.
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]

name: Decline chair push up diamond
instructions:
1. Begin by placing your feet on a chair and positioning your hands on the floor close together, forming a diamond shape with your thumbs and index fingers.
2. Engage your core and ensure your body forms a straight line from your head to your heels.
3. Lower your chest towards your hands by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your body.
4. Push through your hands to return to the starting position, straightening your arms. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Keep your core engaged throughout the exercise to maintain a straight body line and avoid sagging your hips.
2. Ensure your hands are positioned directly under your chest when you lower yourself.
3. Use a stable chair or bench to prevent any movement or slipping during the exercise.
4. Breathe steadily throughout the exercise, exhaling as you push up and inhaling as you lower down.
5. Avoid flaring your elbows out to the sides; keep them close to your body to engage your triceps more effectively.

name: Decline levitating sit ups bodyweight
instructions:
1. Begin by lying on a decline bench with your feet secured under the footpads. Position your torso so it is parallel to the floor, 'levitating' off the bench.
2. Place your hands behind your head with your elbows pointing out to the sides.
3. Engage your core and lift your upper body towards your knees, keeping your back straight and avoiding pulling on your neck.
4. Lower your upper body back to the starting position with control, maintaining the parallel position. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Breathe steadily throughout the exercise, exhaling as you lift your shoulders and inhaling as you lower them.
2. Ensure your lower back remains off the bench and parallel to the floor to engage your core muscles effectively.
3. Use a stable decline bench to prevent any movement or slipping during the exercise.
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]

name: Diamond push up
instructions:
1. Begin in a plank position with your hands close together under your chest, fingers forming a diamond shape (thumbs and index fingers touching).
2. Lower your body towards the floor, keeping your elbows close to your body and your back straight.
3. Pause briefly as your chest nearly touches your hands.
4. Push back up to the starting plank position, maintaining a straight line from your head to your heels throughout the movement.
tips:
1. Ensure your elbows do not flare out; they should stay close to your body to maximize triceps engagement.
2. Control your descent and ascent to increase muscle tension and enhance strength gains.
3. Breathe in as you lower down and exhale forcefully as you push back up to help power the movement.
4. Keep your head in a neutral position, aligned with your spine, to avoid neck strain.
5. Perform the push-up at a slow to moderate pace to focus on form and muscle engagement without rushing.

common_mistakes: ["Hands positioned too far forward", "Elbows flaring out", "Hips sagging", "Not touching hands"]

name: Donkey kicks bodyweight
instructions:
1. Begin on all fours in a tabletop position with your knees hip-width apart on the floor, hands under your shoulders, and your back flat.
2. Keeping your knee bent at a 90-degree angle, lift your right leg up towards the ceiling, driving your heel upward as if pushing against the sky.
3. Push your leg up until your thigh is in line with your body and your foot is above your knee.
4. Lower your leg slowly back to the starting position without letting your knee touch the floor. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions before switching to the left leg.
tips:
1. Ensure your movements are controlled and deliberate to maximize glute activation and minimize momentum.
2. Keep your neck and spine in a neutral position by gazing down at the floor.
3. Avoid swinging your leg or using jerky movements; the power should come from your glutes and not from momentum.

common_mistakes: ["Arching lower back to get height", "Not squeezing glute at top", "Swinging leg with momentum", "Letting hips rotate open"]

name: Drag curl dumbbell
instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with an underhand grip (palms facing up). Let your arms hang straight down by your sides.
2. Begin by dragging the dumbbells upward along your torso by bending your elbows and pulling them back, as if you are zipping up your jacket.
3. Continue lifting the dumbbells until they reach chest height, focusing on squeezing your biceps at the top of the movement.
4. Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the descent. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Focus on the mind-muscle connection, feeling the contraction in your biceps throughout the movement.
2. Use a weight that allows you to perform the exercise with proper form without straining.
3. Avoid using momentum; perform the exercise in a slow and controlled manner.

name: Drag curl Resistance band
instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and place a resistance band under your feet. Hold the handles of the band with both hands, palms facing forward.
2. Keep your elbows close to your body and your back straight. Start with your arms fully extended down at your sides.
3. Curl the handles up by dragging your elbows along your torso, keeping them close to your torso, until your hands reach chest level.
4. Slowly lower the handles back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Exhale as you curl the handles up and inhale as you lower them back down.
2. Use a lighter resistance band if needed to ensure proper form and gradually increase the resistance as you get stronger.
3. Focus on engaging your biceps throughout the movement.

name: Dumbbell ab crunch
instructions:
1. Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart. Hold a dumbbell with both hands striaght over your chest.
2. Lift your head, shoulders, and upper back off the floor by contracting your abdominal muscles.
3. Raise the dumbbell towards the ceiling as you perform the crunch, keeping your arms straight. Pause for a moment at the top of the movement, squeezing your abs.
4. Slowly lower your upper body back to the starting position, keeping control of the movement. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
secondary_muscles: ["Shoulders (Deltoids), Hip Flexors (Iliopsoas), Obliques (Internal and External Obliques)"]
tips:
1. Keep your lower back pressed against the floor throughout the exercise to avoid straining it.
2. Avoid using your neck muscles to lift your head; focus on using your abdominal muscles.
3. Breathe out as you lift your upper body and breathe in as you lower it.

name: Dumbbell armpit row
instructions:
1. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your arms fully extended and palms facing your body.
2. Pull the dumbbells up towards your armpits by bending your elbows. Your elbows should be pointing out to the sides.
3. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position in a controlled manner. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Keep your back straight and avoid rounding your shoulders during the exercise.
2. Maintain a slight bend in your knees to reduce strain on your lower back.
3. Breathe out as you lift the dumbbells and breathe in as you lower them.

name: Dumbbell Incline Bench Press
instructions:
1. Sit on an incline bench set at a 45-degree angle, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your arms bent and dumbbells positioned near your chest.
2. Press both dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended, keeping your palms facing forward.
3. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control.
4. Repeat the pressing motion for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Ensure your wrists remain straight and aligned with your forearms.
2. Avoid locking your elbows at the top of the press to protect your joints.
3. Use a weight that allows for proper form and control.

name: Dumbbell Incline Shrug
instructions:
1. Set an incline bench to a 45-degree angle and sit back against it, holding a dumbbell in each hand with your arms extended at your sides, palms facing each other.
2. Shrug your shoulders upward, lifting the dumbbells towards your ears, without bending your elbows.
3. Slowly lower your shoulders back to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Exhale as you shrug your shoulders upward and inhale as you lower them.
2. Focus on lifting your shoulders straight up and down, avoiding any circular or forward motion.
3. Use a slow and controlled motion to maximize muscle engagement and avoid swinging.

name: Dumbbell Lying Supine Curl
instructions:
1. Lie on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand, arms fully extended towards the floor by your sides, and palms facing your thighs.
2. Curl the dumbbells by bending your elbows until your forearms are parallel to the floor while rotating the dumbbells to face backward.
3. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control, fully extending your arms. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Keep your upper arms stationary and only move your forearms to ensure maximum bicep engagement.
2. Avoid using momentum; focus on a slow and controlled motion throughout the exercise.
3. Use a weight that allows for proper form and control throughout the exercise.

name: Exercise Ball Back Extension With Hands Behind Head
instructions:
1. Position yourself face down on an exercise ball with your hips resting on the ball and your feet anchored against a wall or stable surface for balance.
2. Place your hands behind your head, elbows pointing out to the sides.
3. Engage your core and lower your upper body towards the floor, rounding your back slightly.
4. Lift your upper body back up until your body forms a straight line from your head to your hips. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
secondary_muscles: ["Glutes (gluteus maximus, gluteus mideus), Hamstrings (Biceps Femoris, Semitendinosus, Semimembranosus)"]

3. Ensure your feet are firmly anchored to prevent slipping and maintain balance.
4. Avoid overextending your back at the top of the movement to prevent strain.
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]

name: High cable kneeling crunch
instructions:
1. Attach a rope handle to a high pulley on a cable machine. Kneel down facing the machine, holding the rope handles with both hands, and position them close to your head.
2. Keep your back straight and engage your core muscles topull the rope down by crunching your torso towards your knees, bringing your elbows towards your thighs.
3. Just before your elbows touch your thighs, return to the starting position with control, keeping the tension on the cable.
4. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Ensure your knees are firmly planted on the floor for stability.
2. Keep your hips stable and avoid using momentum.
3. Exhale as you crunch down and inhale as you return to the starting position.

name: Kettlebell Sumo Deadlift with High Pull
instructions:
1. Place a kettlebell on the floor between your feet. Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart and your toes pointed slightly outward. 
2. Hinge at your hips and bend your knees to lower your body, reaching down to grasp the kettlebell handle with both hands, keeping your back straight and chest up.
3. Push through your heels to lift the kettlebell off the floor, extending your hips and knees simultaneously. As you reach the standing position, explosively pull the kettlebell upward towards your chin by bending your elbows and keeping them pointed outward.
4. Lower the kettlebell back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Pec deck fly machine
instructions:

2. Squeeze your chest muscles to bring the handles together in front of your chest.
3. Slowly return to the starting position with control, bringing your elbows back to your sides. 
4. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Keep your back pressed against the backrest throughout the exercise to maintain proper form.
2. Focus on engaging your chest muscles throughout the movement.
3. Exhale as you bring your elbows together and inhale as you return them to the starting position.

name: Plank on elbows
instructions:
1. Begin by lying face down on the floor. Place your elbows directly under your shoulders and your forearms flat on the floor, parallel to each other.
2. Engage your core and lift your body off the floor, forming a straight line from your head to your heels. Your elbows and toes should support your body weight.
3. Hold this position, keeping your core tight and your body straight. Avoid letting your hips sag or rise too high.
4. Maintain the plank position for the desired amount of time, focusing on your form and breathing steadily.
tips:
1. Engage your core throughout the exercise to maintain stability and support your lower back.
2. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels, avoiding any sagging or lifting of the hips.
3. Keep your shoulders directly above your elbows to maintain proper alignment and reduce strain on your shoulders.

name: Seated hip abductor machine
instructions:

2. Grip the side handles to stabilize your upper body and press your legs outward against the resistance, pushing the pads apart in a controlled motion.
3. Pause for a second at the peak of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
4. Repeat for the desired repetitions.
tips:

2. Focus on squeezing your glutes and outer thighs throughout the movement.
3. Perform the movement slowly and avoid using momentum to push the legs outward.

instructions:
1. Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and your legs fully extended. Adjust the backrest and leg pads so the pad rests just above your heels.
2. Pull your heels down by bending your knees until the pad reaches down as far as possible.
3. Pause for a second, then slowly return to the starting position. 
4. Repeat for the desired repetitions.
tips:
1. Exhale as you curl and inhale as you return to the starting position.
2. Do not let the weight stack drop; control the descent movement for maximum muscle activation.

name: Side plank
instructions:
1. Lie on your right side with your legs extended straight and stacked on top of each other. Prop yourself up on your right elbow, keeping your elbow directly under your shoulder.
2. Engage your core and lift your hips off the floor, forming a straight line from your head to your feet.
3. Hold the position for the desired duration, keeping your body straight and your core tight.
4. Slowly lower your hips back to the starting position with control. Repeat on the other side by lying on your left side and following the same steps.
tips:
1. Ensure your elbow is directly under your shoulder to support your upper body.
2. Focus on keeping your head in line with your spine to prevent neck strain.
3. Maintain a straight body line, avoiding any sagging or lifting of the hips.
4. Perform the exercise on a comfortable, non-slip surface to ensure safety and reduce discomfort.

name: Side push up
instructions:
1. Lie on your right side with your legs bent and stacked on top of each other. Place your right arm across your chest and your left hand on the floor in front of you for support.
2. Engage your core and press your left hand into the floor, lifting your upper body off the floor.
3. Hold the top position for a moment, feeling the contraction in your chest and triceps.
4. Slowly lower your upper body back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, then switch to your left side and perform the exercise with your right hand for support.
tips:
1. Breathe steadily, exhaling as you push up and inhaling as you lower back down.
2. Ensure your supporting hand is firmly placed on the floor to provide stability.
3. Avoid using momentum to lift your upper body; focus on using your chest and triceps muscles.

name: Star crunches
instructions:
1. Lie flat on your back with your arms and legs extended out to form an "X" shape. Keep your core engaged and your lower back pressed into the floor.
2. Crunch your abdominal muscles to lift your shoulders and legs off the floor and lift your arms and legs towards each other, reaching your hands towards your ankles.
3. Lower your arms and legs back to the starting position with control.
4. Repeat the movement for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Breathe steadily: exhale as you lift your arm and leg, and inhale as you return to the starting position.
2. Focus on using your abdominal muscles to lift your arms and legs off the floor.
3. Use a mat to provide cushioning and support for your back.

name: Stationary Exercise Bike
instructions:
1. Adjust the seat height so that when you sit on the bike, your feet can comfortably reach the pedals with a slight bend in your knees at the bottom of the pedal stroke.
2. Sit on the bike with your back straight, and grip the handlebars lightly. Place your feet securely on the pedals, ensuring they are aligned properly.

4. Adjust the resistance level using the bike's control knob or digital settings to match your desired intensity. Continue pedaling for the duration of your workout.

1. Maintain good posture by keeping your back straight and shoulders relaxed to avoid strain.
2. Start with a lower resistance level if you are a beginner and gradually increase it as you build strength and endurance.

name: Suspension Trainer with Grips Pike
instructions:
1. Adjust the suspension trainer handles to about knee height. Place your feet in the handles and assume a plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, placed directly under your shoulders, and arms slightly bent.
2. Engage your core and maintain a straight line from your head to your heels.
3. Keeping your legs straight, lift your hips towards the ceiling, forming an inverted V-shape with your body.
4. Slowly lower your hips back to the starting position with control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
tips:
1. Breathe steadily: exhale as you lift your hips and inhale as you lower them back down.
2. Focus on using your core muscles to lift your hips, avoiding excessive use of your arms and legs.
3. Start with a smaller range of motion and gradually increase as your strength and flexibility improve.

1. Adjust the suspension trainer handles to about knee height. Place your feet in the handles and assume a push-up position with your hands placed directly under your shoulders, arms fully extended, and body in a straight line from head to heels.

3. Push back up to the starting position and rotate your body to the right as you reach the top, extending your right arm towards the ceiling to enter a side plank position.

2. Focus on smooth transitions between the push-up and side plank for fluid motion.
3. Maintain even breathing to help keep your movements controlled and steady.

name: V crunches
instructions:

3. Squeeze your abdominal muscles at the top of the movement, ensuring your hands reach towards your feet.

name: Dumbbell Flyes
description: An isolation exercise that stretches and contracts the chest muscles.
instructions:
1. Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, arms extended above chest.
2. With a slight bend in your elbows, lower the dumbbells out to the sides.
3. Lower until you feel a stretch in your chest.
4. Squeeze your chest to bring the dumbbells back together.
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]
common_mistakes: ["Bending elbows too much (turns into press)", "Going too heavy and losing form", "Not feeling chest stretch at bottom", "Letting weights crash together at top"]

name: Dumbbell Incline Flyes
description: Targets the upper chest with a fly movement on an incline.
instructions:
1. Set the bench to a 30-45 degree incline.
2. Hold dumbbells above your chest with arms extended.
3. Lower the dumbbells out to the sides with slightly bent elbows.
4. Squeeze your chest to bring them back together.
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]
common_mistakes: ["Bench too steep", "Arms too straight (shoulder stress)", "Not enough stretch at bottom", "Using momentum"]

name: Dumbbell Squeeze Press
description: Pressing while squeezing dumbbells together for maximum chest activation.
instructions:
1. Lie on a flat bench holding two dumbbells together above your chest.
2. Press the dumbbells together throughout the movement.
3. Lower them to your chest while maintaining the squeeze.
4. Press back up while keeping dumbbells pressed together.
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]
common_mistakes: ["Letting dumbbells separate", "Losing the squeeze at bottom or top", "Going too heavy", "Rushing the movement"]

name: Dumbbell Floor Press
description: A bench press variation performed on the floor for limited range of motion.
instructions:
1. Lie on the floor with knees bent and a dumbbell in each hand.
2. Start with arms extended above your chest.
3. Lower until your upper arms touch the floor.
4. Press back up to the starting position.
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]
common_mistakes: ["Bouncing elbows off floor", "Losing shoulder blade retraction", "Arching lower back excessively", "Not pausing at bottom"]

name: Single Arm Dumbbell Bench Press
description: Unilateral pressing for core stability and muscle balance.
instructions:
1. Lie on a bench with one dumbbell in one hand.
2. Press the dumbbell up while bracing your core.
3. Lower with control, keeping your body stable.
4. Complete all reps on one side, then switch.
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]
common_mistakes: ["Dumbbells drifting apart at bottom", "Not going deep enough", "Unstable shoulder position"]

name: Close Grip Dumbbell Press
description: A narrow grip press that emphasizes the inner chest and triceps.
instructions:
1. Lie on a bench with dumbbells held close together above your chest.
2. Keep the dumbbells touching or close throughout the movement.
3. Lower to your chest with elbows tucked in.
4. Press back up to the starting position.
form_tips: ["Start on hands and knees", "Keep knee bent at 90 degrees", "Lift knee out to side (abduction)", "Keep hips and torso stable", "Squeeze glute at top"]
